summary: Parallel adversarial audit of the Vistas fund data pipeline: scheme/stock identity, attribution sanity, viz correctness, NAV bridge

synth.headline: Bedrock is sound: scope of the re-code defect is exactly the 2 known splits (no hidden third), plus a small set of fixable stock-identity leaks — build on it after the listed fixes.
confirmed_findings: HIGH | Two confirmed re-code splits (Kotak Large Cap 1223->262, Canara Robeco ELSS 10291->456) double-count in attribution NOW; exhaustively proven NO third case exists | Merge each pair under the long-history code (keep 1223/10291 as canonical, append the 3 successor months from 262/456), delete the successor attribution files, recompute the merged skill over the full ~151-month series, and install a code->canonical alias map {262:1223, 456:10291} at attribution-build time so the frozen old codes stop missing Aug/Sep/Oct-2025.

HIGH | Mis-split / silent return-join drop: 16 equity vst_ids exist in holdings but have NO row in tr_returns_monthly, so ~15.5k holdings rows (Rs ~583k MV, ~0.33% of equity MV) across 388 schemes silently fall out of the return join — dominated by VST04239 Tata Motors-DVR (8,273 rows, 246 schemes), VST00896 (5,494 rows, 284 schemes), VST04258 Techno Electric Merged (1,321 rows, 50 schemes) | Build/attach a return series for each missing vst_id (map DVR/merged/renamed tickers to their continuing return), or fail loudly when a held vst_id has no return rather than dropping it silently from per-stock attribution.

MEDIUM | Mis-merge cross-company contamination: VST02743 carries both Genus Power (correct, GENUSPOWER) and 30 rows of Genus Paper & Boards, a different company that inherits the wrong return; symmetric Kirloskar case where ISIN INE146L01010 (Kirloskar Oil Engines) is also attached to VST03245 Kirloskar Industries, mis-pricing 28 Oil-Engines rows in both directions | Split the contaminated vst_ids so each maps to exactly one issuer/return series; add an ISIN<->vst_id 1:1 invariant check (the data shows ~8 ISINs mapping to >1 vst_id and ~21 vst_ids mapping to >1 ISIN — most are same-entity reissues, but flag and resolve the cross-company ones).

MEDIUM | 32 Arbitrage-Fund codes appear in only ONE month (2025-08) — a one-month ingestion artifact, all 32 carry attribution files despite n_months=1 and being debt-like (not equity-skill universe) | Exclude sebi_category='Arbitrage Fund' from the equity attribution universe and gate out any code with n_months<6 from skill scoring; investigate why the 2025-08 feed briefly emitted them so it doesn't recur on format shifts.

LOW | SBI / Sundaram 'Series I/II/III' closed-end ELSS clones share near-identical portfolios (Jaccard up to 1.0) but are legally distinct schemes — must NOT be auto-merged | Keep all 6 codes distinct; require BOTH high holdings overlap AND identical normalized name AND a stop->start temporal boundary (no overlap) before any automated identity merge, so the clones (which overlap in time) are never collapsed.

LOW | Nippon '(SP n)' segregated-portfolio side-pockets share a fund name with the live equity code but hold a different (credit) book (Jaccard 0.0) — correctly distinct, flag to block name-based merge | Treat (SP n) codes as separate non-equity instruments (already excluded from attribution); ensure name-normalization respects/strips the '(SP n)' token so a side-pocket is never folded into the parent equity scheme.

INFO | 9 codes (intl/debt/retirement, mostly stopping 2024-03) have no attribution file — survivorship/universe exit, not a re-code | No merge needed; document these as deliberate universe exits so the 777-holdings vs 768-attribution gap is explained rather than mistaken for a matching bug.
recommended_fix_order: 1. Apply the code->canonical alias map {262:1223, 456:10291}: merge the 2 split pairs, append the 3 successor months onto the long-history codes, delete successor attribution, recompute merged skill over the full series (unblocks the most-recent 3 months on the only two 'skilled' verdicts that are currently stranded).

2. Close the silent return-join drop: give the 16 missing vst_ids a return series (DVR/merged/renamed mappings) and make a held-but-unjoined vst_id raise rather than vanish — biggest per-stock attribution distortion by row/scheme count.
3. Resolve the cross-company identity contaminations (VST02743 Genus, Kirloskar INE146L01010) and add the ISIN<->vst_id 1:1 invariant check to catch future mis-merge/mis-split.
4. Exclude Arbitrage Fund category and gate n_months<6 out of skill scoring; trace the 2025-08 arbitrage ingestion so it doesn't recur.

5. Harden the identity-merge guard (require name + holdings-overlap + temporal stop->start boundary) so SBI/Sundaram clones and Nippon (SP n) side-pockets are never auto-merged; document the 9 universe-exit codes to close the 777-vs-768 reconciliation.

audited.summary: The holdings store has 777 navindia_codes; 727 reach the global max month 2025-10. Exhaustively checking all four mechanisms (latest name+amc match, boundary re-code with name+holdings overlap, full-overlap dups, ISIN/holdings-proven renames), I find EXACTLY 2 true re-code splits — and they are precisely the two already KNOWN: Kotak Large Cap (old code 1223 -> successor 262) and Canara Robeco ELSS Tax Saver (old 10291 -> successor 456). Both old codes freeze at 2025-07 (148 months) and an identically-named new code starts 2025-08 with 3 months, top-15 vst_id Jaccard = 0.875 across the boundary, identical sebi_category. No OTHER re-codes exist at any month boundary. The known scope is therefore COMPLETE — there is no hidden third case. However the audit surfaces three adjacent identity hazards that are NOT re-codes but will fool a naive 'distinct scheme' count: (1) 32 single-month Arbitrage-Fund codes that appear ONLY in 2025-08 (a one-month ingestion artifact, not equity, all 32 sit in attribution); (2) SBI/Sundaram 'Series I/II/III' closed-end ELSS clones that hold near-identical portfolios (Jaccard up to 1.0) but are legally distinct schemes with their own codes — must NOT be merged; (3) Nippon '(SP 1)' segregated-portfolio side-pocket codes that share a fund name with the live equity code but hold a totally different (credit) book (Jaccard 0.0) — correctly distinct. DOUBLE-COUNT IMPACT NOW: attribution has 768 files; the 2 split pairs are each present TWICE (1223+262, 10291+456), so attribution lists 766 distinct real schemes as 768 entries. The successor entries (262, 456) carry only 3 months and verdict 'insufficient history' (excess_cagr=None), so they do not corrupt the skill leaderboard — but the FULL-history skill verdict ('skilled', excess_cagr 2.07%/3.18%) is stranded on the frozen old codes (1223/10291) which now MISS the most recent 3 months of data. Best count of truly distinct schemes in the holdings store: 775 (777 minus the 2 redundant successors).
audited.metrics: total navindia_codes=777; reach 2025-10=727; early-stoppers (last_ym<2025-10)=50, of which single-month=39 (32 @ 2025-08 arbitrage artifact + 7 @ 2025-10 genuinely-new funds) and genuine multi-month stoppers=18. Stop-month histogram for the 18 genuine stoppers: 2021-05:1, 2024-03:9, 2024-07:1, 2024-12:1, 2025-01:1, 2025-02:2, 2025-06:1, 2025-07:2 (the two 2025-07 = the split-old codes 1223 & 10291). Mechanism(a) exact latest name+amc shared by >1 code: 2 groups (1223/262, 10291/456). Mechanism(b) boundary re-code by name+holdings-Jaccard: same 2, J=0.875 each (next-best name-identical match none; arbitrage cross-matches J<=0.5 are spurious large-cap overlap). Mechanism(c) full-overlap dups: 0 true (SBI/Sundaram series are clones but distinct schemes). Mechanism(d) renames: 0. Attribution files=768; codes w/o attribution=9 (intl/debt/retirement funds last seen 2024-03). Split successors n_months: 262=3, 456=3 (verdict 'insufficient history'); split olds: 1223=147, 10291=148 (verdict 'skilled'). Distinct schemes: holdings=775, attribution=766.

findings.title: Two confirmed re-code splits (Kotak Large Cap, Canara Robeco ELSS) double-count in attribution NOW; no third case exists
findings.evidence: Exact latest name+amc match AND boundary holdings match both isolate exactly 2 groups: 1223(2013-04..2025-07,148mo)->262(2025-08..2025-10,3mo) and 10291(2013-04..2025-07,148mo)->456(2025-08..2025-10,3mo); top-15 vst_id Jaccard across the 2025-07/08 boundary = 0.875 for both, identical sebi_category (Large Cap / ELSS). All 4 codes have separate attribution JSON files, so each fund is counted TWICE. Successor attribution (262,456) carries n_months=3, verdict='insufficient history', excess_cagr=None; full-history skill ('skilled', excess_cagr 0.0207 / 0.0318) lives only on the FROZEN old codes 1223/10291 which now stop at 2025-07 and miss Aug/Sep/Oct-2025.
findings.affected: 2 schemes / 4 codes / 768 attribution files (-> 766 distinct)
findings.fix: Merge each pair under one canonical code (keep the long-history code 1223/10291 as the identity, append the 3 successor months from 262/456 onto it), then DELETE the successor attribution files and recompute the merged scheme's skill metrics over the full 151-month series. A code->canonical alias map (e.g. {262:1223, 456:10291}) applied at attribution-build time prevents recurrence. This matches pending task #43.

findings.title: 32 Arbitrage-Fund codes appear in only ONE month (2025-08) — a one-month ingestion artifact polluting attribution
findings.evidence: 32 codes (e.g. 6061 HDFC Arbitrage, 3688 ICICI Pru Arbitrage, 2687 Kotak Arbitrage, 11682 Nippon Arbitrage) all have first_ym=last_ym=2025-08, n_months=1, sebi_category='Arbitrage Fund'. They were ingested for Aug-2025 only then vanish in Sep/Oct. All 32 have attribution files. Arbitrage funds are debt-like / not part of the equity-skill universe; a 1-month snapshot cannot support any skill metric.
findings.affected: 32 codes (all single-month, all in attribution)
findings.fix: Exclude sebi_category='Arbitrage Fund' from the equity attribution universe (they were never meant to be ingested), or at minimum gate out any code with n_months<6 from skill scoring. Investigate why the 2025-08 feed briefly emitted them so it does not recur each time the feed format shifts.

findings.title: SBI / Sundaram 'Series I/II/III' closed-end ELSS clones share near-identical portfolios (Jaccard up to 1.0) but are legally distinct schemes — do NOT merge
findings.evidence: SBI Long Term Advantage Ser I(28845)/II(29348)/III(31927) and Sundaram LT Tax Advantage Sr I(29326)/II(31594)/III(35456) each have distinct codes, inception dates and life-spans; top-15 vst_id Jaccard between siblings reaches 0.765-1.0 (parallel-managed clones). A holdings-overlap-only de-dup would wrongly collapse them.
findings.affected: 6 codes across 2 AMCs (2 clone families)
findings.fix: Keep all 6 as distinct schemes. Add a guard so any future automated identity-merge requires BOTH high holdings overlap AND identical normalized name AND a stop->start boundary (no temporal overlap) before merging — the clones fail the name/boundary test, so they are safe.

findings.title: Nippon '(SP 1)' segregated-portfolio side-pockets share a fund name with the live equity code but hold a different (credit) book — correctly distinct, flag to avoid name-based merge
findings.evidence: 40866 'Nippon India Equity Savings (SP 1)' (2019-09..2024-03) vs live 30520 'Nippon India Equity Savings' share the normalized name but top-15 vst_id Jaccard=0.0 across all shared months — the SP1 code is a segregated side-pocket (credit default carve-out), not the equity portfolio. Same pattern for 40858 (Equity Hybrid SP1) and 41268 (Hybrid Bond SP1). None reach 2025-10; none have attribution files.
findings.affected: 3 codes (Nippon SP-n)
findings.fix: Treat (SP n) codes as separate non-equity instruments (already excluded from attribution). Ensure any name-normalization merge strips/respects the '(SP n)' token so a side-pocket is never folded into the parent equity scheme.

findings.title: 9 codes (intl/debt/retirement) silently dropped at 2024-03 with no attribution — survivorship gap, not a re-code
findings.evidence: 9 of 777 codes have no attribution file: 15037 ICICI US Bluechip, 27895 Nippon Japan, 30863 Nippon US Eq Opp, 42431 Nippon Taiwan, 39632/39754 Retirement-Debt, 40858/40866 Nippon SP1, 41268 Nippon Hybrid Bond. Most stop at 2024-03 (feed dropped overseas/debt funds). They are genuinely distinct schemes that left the universe, not duplicates.
findings.affected: 9 codes
findings.fix: No merge needed. Document these as deliberate universe exits so a future 'distinct scheme' reconciliation (777 holdings codes vs 768 attribution) is explained and not mistaken for a matching bug.

verdict.reason: Independently reproduced every load-bearing claim in SI-1 on the raw data; it holds up fully. (1) HOLDINGS boundaries: navindia_code is stored as STRING. Code 1223 = Kotak Large Cap Fund (IDCW), Kotak Mahindra MF, Large Cap Fund, 148 monthly snapshots 2013-04..2025-07; successor 262 = same name/amc/sebi_category, 3 snapshots 2025-08..2025-10. Code 10291 = Canara Robeco ELSS Tax Saver (IDCW), Canara Robeco MF, ELSS, 148mo 2013-04..2025-07; successor 456 = same identity, 3mo 2025-08..2025-10. Exact match on name+amc+sebi_category for both pairs. (2) Top-15 vst_id Jaccard across the 2025-07/2025-08 boundary = 14/16 = 0.875 for BOTH pairs — reproduced to the digit. (3) Attribution JSONs: 262 and 456 carry n_months=3, verdict='insufficient history', excess_cagr=None, t_stat=None; the full-history 'skilled' verdict with excess_cagr 0.0207 (1223) / 0.0318 (10291) lives ONLY on the frozen old codes, which stop at 2025-07 and miss Aug/Sep/Oct-2025. (4) All four codes have separate attribution files (and all four appear in _manifest.json), so each fund is counted twice — 768 distinct attribution json files (excluding _manifest.json). (5) NO THIRD CASE — the most refutable claim — verified THREE independent ways: only 2 codes freeze at exactly 2025-07 (10291, 1223); a general (name,amc)-duplicate scan across all 196 snapshots returns exactly these 2 pairs and nothing else; and a relaxed normalized-name matcher (strips IDCW/Growth/plan suffixes and parentheticals, allows the successor to start at ANY later month) scanning all 50 codes that don't reach the latest snapshot (2025-10) still surfaces only these 2 pairs. The other 40 codes that start at 2025-08 (and 50 that end before 2025-10) are genuine new schemes / terminations with no same-AMC same-name twin, not re-codes. Only one immaterial nuance: the old-code attribution n_months is 147 (Kotak) / 148 (Canara) = the monthly-RETURN series length, whereas the finding's '148mo' refers to holdings snapshots; both are internally consistent and change no conclusion. Evidence files: data/funds/history/holdings_history.parquet; data/funds_attribution/{1223,262,10291,456}.json and _manifest.json.

audited.summary: vst_id identity is MOSTLY clean but has a small set of real contaminations that corrupt the return join. Coverage is excellent: 99.43% of equity rows and 99.93% of equity market value carry a non-null vst_id. nse_symbol is perfectly stable (zero vst_ids carry >1 symbol). The defects are concentrated in two patterns, both at the company-identity boundary. (1) MIS-MERGE: 15 vst_ids map to >1 ISIN, but only 4 cross a genuinely different issuer-family; of those, ONE is a true cross-company contamination — VST02743 carries both Genus Power (correct, the return series is GENUSPOWER) and 30 rows of Genus Paper & Boards, a different small company that inherits the wrong return. The others (Cohance/Suven Pharma, Kirloskar, Power Grid) are ISIN reissues of the same entity. (2) MIS-SPLIT (more damaging): 5 ISINs map to 2 vst_ids each. The worst is INE146L01010 (Kirloskar Oil Engines' ISIN), which is also attached to VST03245 = Kirloskar Industries — a different company whose return series (KIRLOSIND) then mis-prices 28 Oil-Engines rows; this case is contaminated in BOTH directions. The biggest VALUE leak is the silent-drop class: 16 equity vst_ids exist in holdings but have NO row in tr_returns_monthly, so 15,454 holdings rows (Rs 582k MV, 0.33% of equity MV) across 388 schemes silently fall out of the return join — dominated by Tata Motors-DVR (VST04239, 8,255 rows, 246 schemes) and Techno Electric Merged (VST04258, 1,312 rows, 49 schemes). None of these is catastrophic in aggregate, but for a bedrock "no score for error" audit each is a concrete, fixable identity defect that distorts per-stock attribution.
audited.metrics: Equity rows=3,125,333. vst_id coverage: 3,107,565 rows non-null (99.431%); MV-weighted 99.934% (missing-MV Rs 118,686 of Rs 179,119,314). Missing-vst equity rows=17,768 (12,373 have a valid INE ISIN -> recoverable; 0 have a symbol). (a) vst_id->multi-ISIN: 15 vst_ids; only 4 cross issuer-family; 1 true cross-company (VST02743). (b) ISIN->multi-vst_id: 5 ISINs (INE285K01026, INE146L01010, INE155A01022, INE495B01038, INE726V01018). (d) nse_symbol drift: 0 vst_ids with >1 symbol. Silent return-join drop: 16 equity vst_ids absent from tr_returns -> 15,454 rows / Rs 582,342 MV / 0.325% eq MV / 388 schemes. tr_returns keys on vst_id (1,570 vst_ids, 276,970 rows).

findings.title: Mis-split: Tata Motors-DVR (VST04239) has no return series -> 8,255 rows silently dropped from skill join
findings.evidence: ISIN INE155A01022 maps to VST04238 (Tata Motors Ltd, in tr_returns) AND VST04239 (Tata Motors-DVR, NOT in tr_returns). VST04239 = 8,255 equity rows, Rs 367,312 MV, 246 schemes — all drop from the return join because no ret_1m exists for that vst_id.
findings.affected: 246 schemes; 8,255 holdings rows; Rs 367k MV
findings.fix: Map VST04239 (Tata Motors-DVR) to its own DVR total-return series, or fold DVR holdings into VST04238 (ordinary) if DVR converted to ordinary on the 2024 cancellation — then backfill ret_1m for VST04239 so the rows join.

findings.title: Silent return-join drop class: 16 equity vst_ids in holdings absent from tr_returns_monthly
findings.evidence: Holdings carry 1,583 distinct equity vst_ids; tr_returns_monthly has only the matching subset. 16 of them have NO return row, so 15,454 holdings rows / Rs 582,342 MV (0.325% of equity MV) across 388 schemes get no ret_1m and are excluded from every skill/attribution number. Dominated by VST04239 (Tata Motors-DVR), VST04258 (Techno Electric Merged), VST03777 (Pricol Merged).
findings.affected: 388 schemes; 15,454 rows; 0.325% of equity MV
findings.fix: Generate tr_returns coverage for these 16 vst_ids (or alias the *(Merged)/DVR* vst_ids onto their surviving entity's series). Add a pipeline gate: assert every equity vst_id present in holdings has >=1 row in tr_returns, else flag the scheme.

findings.title: True cross-company mis-split contamination: INE146L01010 (Kirloskar Oil Engines) also mapped to VST03245 (Kirloskar Industries)
findings.evidence: INE146L01010 is Kirloskar Oil Engines' ISIN and maps to VST03243 (Oil Engines, correct, 2,667 rows). It is ALSO mapped to VST03245 = Kirloskar Industries Ltd (a DIFFERENT company; VST03245's return series in tr_returns is KIRLOSIND). 28 Oil-Engines holding rows (5 schemes) thus resolve to the wrong vst_id and inherit Kirloskar Industries' returns. Contaminated in BOTH (a) and (b) directions.
findings.affected: 5 schemes; 28 rows (Oil Engines mis-keyed) + VST03245 also carries INE250A01039
findings.fix: Split the two Kirloskar entities cleanly: INE146L01010 -> VST03243 only (Oil Engines); INE250A01039 -> VST03245 only (Industries). Re-key the 28 contaminated rows to VST03243.

findings.title: True cross-company mis-merge: VST02743 carries both Genus Power and Genus Paper & Boards
findings.evidence: VST02743 maps to INE955D01029 (Genus Power Infrastructures, the return series = GENUSPOWER, correct) AND INE949P01018 (Genus Paper & Boards Ltd — a different, tiny company). 30 Genus-Paper rows (1 scheme, 2014-2017, Rs 0.3 MV) wrongly inherit Genus Power's returns.
findings.affected: 1 scheme; 30 rows; Rs 0.3 MV
findings.fix: Assign Genus Paper & Boards (INE949P01018) its own vst_id distinct from VST02743; re-key the 30 rows.

findings.title: Mis-split: Techno Electric *(Merged)* and Pricol *(Merged)* duplicate vst_ids with no returns
findings.evidence: INE285K01026 -> VST00752 (Techno Electric, in tr) + VST04258 (Techno Electric (Merged), NOT in tr; 1,312 rows / 49 schemes / Rs 25,849 MV). INE726V01018 -> VST01322 (Pricol, in tr) + VST03777 (Pricol (Merged), NOT in tr; 76 rows / 6 schemes). The *(Merged)* duplicate vst_ids split one real security into two and drop the merged-labelled rows from returns.
findings.affected: Techno: 49 schemes/1,312 rows; Pricol: 6 schemes/76 rows
findings.fix: Collapse each *(Merged)* vst_id onto the surviving entity vst_id (VST04258->VST00752, VST03777->VST01322) so the same ISIN has one vst_id and the rows pick up returns. Add a build rule: a *(Merged)*-named company must not get a new vst_id distinct from its survivor.

findings.title: Mis-split: Suven Life Sciences vs Suven Pharmaceuticals share ISIN INE495B01038 across two vst_ids
findings.evidence: INE495B01038 maps to VST00135 (Suven Pharmaceuticals, 1,765 rows, 118 schemes) AND VST04204 (Suven Life Sciences, 375 rows, 21 schemes). These are two distinct listed entities (Suven Life Sciences demerged Suven Pharma in 2020); both vst_ids are in tr_returns, so rows split across two return series for what holdings labels as one ISIN. Compounded by VST00135 also carrying Cohance ISIN INE03QK01018 (428 rows, 105 schemes, 2025) after the Cohance rebrand/merger.
findings.affected: Suven split: 139 schemes; Cohance overlap on VST00135: 105 schemes / 428 rows
findings.fix: Resolve the ISIN->entity timeline: INE495B01038 should belong to exactly one entity at a given date. Confirm Suven Pharma->Cohance continuity is intended on VST00135 (entity continuity, likely OK) but ensure VST04204 (Suven Life Sciences) does not share INE495B01038 — re-attribute Life Sciences' rows to its correct ISIN/vst_id.

findings.title: Recoverable missing vst_id: 12,373 equity rows have a valid INE ISIN but null vst_id
findings.evidence: 17,768 equity rows have null vst_id (0.569% of rows; Rs 118,686 MV = 0.066% of eq MV). 12,373 of them carry a valid INE/INF ISIN, so vst_id is recoverable by ISIN. Dominated by partly-paid lines (Reliance Industries Partly Paidup 2,001 rows/Rs 82,172 MV; Grasim Partly Paidup; UPL/Sobha/Aditya Birla Fashion Partly Paidup) and delisted micro-caps with ~0 MV.
findings.affected: ~all schemes holding partly-paid scrips; 12,373 rows recoverable
findings.fix: Backfill vst_id from final_isin_his where INE-valid (especially map Partly-Paidup lines onto the parent equity vst_id). Leave true delisted/zero-MV micro-caps null but mark them excluded-by-design.

finding.title: Mis-split: Tata Motors-DVR (VST04239) has no return series -> 8,255 rows silently dropped from skill join
finding.evidence: ISIN INE155A01022 maps to VST04238 (Tata Motors Ltd, in tr_returns) AND VST04239 (Tata Motors-DVR, NOT in tr_returns). VST04239 = 8,255 equity rows, Rs 367,312 MV, 246 schemes — all drop from the return join because no ret_1m exists for that vst_id.
finding.fix: Map VST04239 (Tata Motors-DVR) to its own DVR total-return series, or fold DVR holdings into VST04238 (ordinary) if DVR converted to ordinary on the 2024 cancellation — then backfill ret_1m for VST04239 so the rows join.

verdict.reason: Independently reproduced every hard number on the data. (1) ISIN INE155A01022 maps to BOTH VST04238 (Tata Motors Ltd, nse_symbol TMPV) and VST04239 (Tata Motors-DVR, nse_symbol TATAMTRDVR) in holdings_history.parquet — confirmed via reported_isin/final_isin_his. (2) In tr_returns_monthly.parquet, VST04238 IS present (252 monthly rows) but VST04239 is COMPLETELY ABSENT (0 rows) — so the DVR vst_id has no return series. (3) VST04239 = exactly 8,255 Equity rows (8,273 incl. 14 Rights + 3 Stock-Future + 1 FRD), market_value sum Rs 367,311.62 (~Rs 367k), held by exactly 246 distinct schemes (navindia_code) — all three figures match the finding to the rupee/row. (4) Date range 2013-04..2024-07 is consistent with the Tata Motors DVR cancellation in 2024 (DVR converted to ordinary), so the missing series is genuinely a real instrument that needs handling. (5) The attribution engine (vistas/funds_attribution.py:99) joins holdings to returns with h.merge(trf, on=['vst_id','fwd_ym'], how='left') keyed on vst_id, so VST04239 gets fwd_ret=NaN. The DVR is a MATERIAL holding (median 1.38% of fund weight, max 9.74%, ~2% of total reported portfolio weight across 8,172 scheme-months), not a rounding error — so its exclusion from skill attribution is genuinely high-severity. The core identity defect, all counts, the MV, the 246-scheme reach, and the no-return-join consequence all reproduce independently.
verdict.corrected: The finding HOLDS — the identity mis-split and all evidence numbers are correct (8,255 equity rows / Rs 367,311.62 MV / 246 schemes / VST04239 absent from tr_returns / VST04238 present). ONE mechanism wording correction: the rows are NOT "silently dropped from the join." The merge is a LEFT join (how='left'), so VST04239 rows survive with fwd_ret=NaN. They are then handled deliberately: cov=fwd_ret.notna() sets covered-weight cw=0 for DVR, and the paper return is renormalised to covered weight (rp = rp_raw / cover) with an explicit code comment "don't penalise for a few unmatched names." So the affected schemes STILL receive attribution — the DVR holding is treated as an UNCOVERED name and reweighted OUT of the return/EW/IC computation, rather than the fund being dropped or penalised. Net effect is still a real defect (a 1.38%-median, up-to-9.74% holding is excluded from skill measurement for up to 246 schemes through 2024-07, biasing rp/IC toward the covered sleeve), so severity 'high' is defensible — but the precise harm is 'systematically excluded as uncovered weight,' not 'rows silently dropped.' Fix as proposed is correct: backfill a DVR total-return series for VST04239 (TATAMTRDVR traded at a structural discount to ordinary, so do NOT naively fold into VST04238's ordinary returns — use the DVR's own price series until the 2024 cancellation, then the conversion ratio).

finding.title: Silent return-join drop class: 16 equity vst_ids in holdings absent from tr_returns_monthly
finding.evidence: Holdings carry 1,583 distinct equity vst_ids; tr_returns_monthly has only the matching subset. 16 of them have NO return row, so 15,454 holdings rows / Rs 582,342 MV (0.325% of equity MV) across 388 schemes get no ret_1m and are excluded from every skill/attribution number. Dominated by VST04239 (Tata Motors-DVR), VST04258 (Techno Electric Merged), VST03777 (Pricol Merged).
finding.fix: Generate tr_returns coverage for these 16 vst_ids (or alias the *(Merged)/DVR* vst_ids onto their surviving entity's series). Add a pipeline gate: assert every equity vst_id present in holdings has >=1 row in tr_returns, else flag the scheme.

verdict.reason: Core claim independently reproduced and CONFIRMED. Mechanism verified in vistas/funds_attribution.py: load_panel() reads tr_returns, drops null-vst_id rows (line 92), then h.merge(trf, on=['vst_id','fwd_ym'], how='left') (line 99); uncovered holdings get fwd_ret=NaN and covered-weight cw=0 (line 105), so they are silently excluded from every skill/attribution number. Reproduced headline numbers: holdings carry 1,583 distinct equity vst_ids (investment_type=='Equity', non-null) vs 1,570 in tr_returns; 16 missing; 15,454 rows; Rs 582,342.17 MV = 0.3251% of equity MV; 388 schemes. Adversarial test: 3 of the missing (VST00896, VST01627, VST03285) DO have ISIN coverage in tr_returns, but those tr rows carry null vst_id, so the vst_id-keyed join still drops them — the finding's join model holds. Materiality is real: max 10.38% of one scheme's equity MV dropped, 49 schemes lose >1%. The proposed fix (generate/alias tr coverage + pipeline gate asserting every equity vst_id has >=1 tr row) is correct. NOT refuting because the substance, mechanism, severity and fix all hold up.
verdict.corrected: Finding holds but three evidence details are wrong and should be corrected. (1) COUNT IS 15, NOT 16, under the pipeline's ACTUAL filter. The attribution code keeps only rows with pct>0 (funds_attribution.py line 86). VST03324 (Uttam Value Steels) appears only via pct=0 rows, so it is pre-filtered out BEFORE the return join and is never 'excluded from skill numbers' for the alleged reason — it is already gone legitimately. The '16' is an artifact of a naive investment_type=='Equity' lens that does not match the pipeline. Under the real filter (equity-substring, non-foreign, pct>0): 15 missing vst_ids, 15,345 rows, Rs 582,339 MV, 0.3253% of equity MV, 388 schemes (scheme count and MV and % are unchanged to 3 figures). (2) ROWS = 15,345, not 15,454, under the pipeline filter. (3) TOP-3 EXEMPLARS ARE MISLABELED. Finding lists Tata Motors-DVR, Techno Electric (Merged), Pricol (Merged). True top-3 by MV: VST04239 Tata Motors-DVR (Rs 367,310), VST00896 Bharti Airtel Ltd Partly Paidup (Rs 187,164) — the SECOND-largest contributor, entirely omitted by the finding — and VST04258 Techno Electric (Merged) (Rs 25,849). Pricol (VST03777, Rs 343) is 6th, ~545x smaller than stated as 3rd. The dominant pattern is corporate-action variants: DVR shares, Partly-Paid rights lines, and *(Merged) entities — which supports the fix recommendation to alias these onto the surviving entity's TR series.

finding.title: True cross-company mis-split contamination: INE146L01010 (Kirloskar Oil Engines) also mapped to VST03245 (Kirloskar Industries)
finding.evidence: INE146L01010 is Kirloskar Oil Engines' ISIN and maps to VST03243 (Oil Engines, correct, 2,667 rows). It is ALSO mapped to VST03245 = Kirloskar Industries Ltd (a DIFFERENT company; VST03245's return series in tr_returns is KIRLOSIND). 28 Oil-Engines holding rows (5 schemes) thus resolve to the wrong vst_id and inherit Kirloskar Industries' returns. Contaminated in BOTH (a) and (b) directions.
finding.affected: 5 schemes; 28 rows (Oil Engines mis-keyed) + VST03245 also carries INE250A01039
finding.fix: Split the two Kirloskar entities cleanly: INE146L01010 -> VST03243 only (Oil Engines); INE250A01039 -> VST03245 only (Industries). Re-key the 28 contaminated rows to VST03243.

verdict.reason: Independently reproduced on data/funds/history/holdings_history.parquet and tr_returns_monthly.parquet. INE146L01010 (Kirloskar Oil Engines' ISIN) resolves (final_isin_his) to TWO vst_ids: VST03243 (KIRLOSENG/KOEL IN Equity, correct, 2,668 rows) AND VST03245 (KIRLOSIND/KKIL IN Equity = Kirloskar Industries, a different listed company). Exactly 28 rows carry the wrong key (final_isin_his=INE146L01010 -> VST03245), across exactly 5 schemes: ITI Multi Cap (40383), ITI ELSS (40684), ITI Balanced Advantage (40963), ITI Small Cap (41070), LIC MF Infrastructure (6876). VST03245 also carries Kirloskar Industries' true ISIN INE250A01039 (3 rows). The two vst_ids hold genuinely different return series (differ in 180 of 181 overlapping months; e.g. Oct-2025 Oil Engines +8.83% vs Industries -3.39%), so the 28 mis-keyed Oil-Engines holdings inherit Kirloskar Industries' returns — a real, material cross-company contamination. The proposed fix (split: INE146L01010 -> VST03243 only; INE250A01039 -> VST03245 only; re-key the 28 rows) is correct. Only a trivial numeric nit: the finding states VST03243 has 2,667 correct rows; the actual count is 2,668 (and reported_isin has 1 additional stray INE146L01010->VST03245 row). This does not change the direction or severity.
verdict.corrected: Finding confirmed with a minor count correction: VST03243 (Oil Engines, correct) holds 2,668 rows on final_isin_his (finding said 2,667). The 28 contaminated rows and 5 schemes are exactly right. Additionally, on reported_isin there is 1 extra stray row (INE146L01010 reported but resolved to VST03245), so the raw mis-resolution is mildly broader than the 28-row final_isin_his count. Severity (high) and the fix stand.

audited.summary: The attribution engine is internally disciplined — verdict logic is consistent (skilled ⟺ t_stat>=2 AND excess_cagr>0, zero contradictions; t in [2.00,4.38], no absurd t>6), short histories correctly degrade to "insufficient history" (187/768), and the verdict distribution is believable and appropriately stingy (only 133/581 finite-t funds = 22.9% clear t>=2; 31 = 5.3% clear t>=3; zero t<=-2). The serious defect is NOT the math but the BENCHMARK ASSIGNMENT: only 5 benchmarks exist, mapped by crude cap-tier, so 32 arbitrage + 19 conservative-hybrid + 25 equity-savings + multi-asset/balanced + all 228 sectoral/thematic funds are benchmarked to NIFTY 500 (pure equity). For hybrid/balanced funds this structurally inflates the equity-sleeve excess: 35 of 133 "skilled" verdicts (26%) are hybrid/multi-asset/balanced funds vs NIFTY 500, including two non-credible claims — Parag Parikh Conservative Hybrid (+23.5%/yr excess, t=2.4, skilled) and Quant Multi Asset (+20.4%/yr, t=2.7, skilled). Secondary: 8 "skilled" verdicts rest on under-3-year histories (n_months 31-35), overstating confidence; 24 gappy histories; the 2 known boundary re-codes confirmed as the ONLY duplicates (both phantom new codes safely degrade to insufficient history). Net: trust the relative ranking within a clean diversified-equity category; do NOT trust skill verdicts for hybrid/arbitrage/thematic funds or for funds with <3yr history.
audited.metrics: 768 schemes scanned. Verdict histogram: "ahead but not yet significant" 365 | "insufficient history" 187 | "skilled" 133 | "lagging benchmark" 80 | "good selector, weak sizer" 2 | "index-like" 1. Finite t_stat: 581 schemes; t>=2: 133 (22.9%); t>=3: 31 (5.3%); t<=-2: 0. excess_cagr: median +2.6%/yr, mean +2.7%, min -71.3%, max +26.0%; >+12%: 8, <-12%: 2. Benchmarks (only 5): NIFTY 100 (Large Cap 34), NIFTY 500 (everything else incl 228 thematic + 32 arbitrage + 19 cons-hybrid + 25 eq-savings), NIFTY LARGEMIDCAP 250 (33), NIFTY MIDCAP 100 (31), NIFTY SMALLCAP 250 (31). skilled in hybrid/arb/balanced cats: 35/133. Duplicate scheme codes: exactly 2 pairs (Kotak Large Cap 1223+262, Canara Robeco ELSS 10291+456). gappy=True: 24. len(ts)==n_months for all 768 (no ts truncation).

findings.title: 35 of 133 'skilled' verdicts are hybrid/multi-asset/balanced funds benchmarked to pure-equity NIFTY 500 — equity-sleeve excess structurally inflated
findings.evidence: Attribution measures the domestic-equity sleeve only (per the 'basis' field) but compares it to the FULL NIFTY 500. Two non-credible extremes both flagged 'skilled': code 42063 Parag Parikh Conservative Hybrid excess_cagr=+0.2351 (23.5%/yr) t=2.39; code 708 Quant Multi Asset Allocation excess_cagr=+0.2040 (20.4%/yr) t=2.70. 35 skilled funds sit in Aggressive Hybrid / Conservative Hybrid / Equity Savings / Multi Asset / Balanced Advantage categories, ALL benched to NIFTY 500. Crosstab confirms every one of these categories maps 100% to NIFTY 500.
findings.affected: 35 of 133 skilled (26%); broader 163 hybrid/arb/balanced/savings schemes all on NIFTY 500
findings.fix: Assign sleeve-appropriate benchmarks by SEBI category: arbitrage/equity-savings/conservative-hybrid -> a debt/T-bill or CRISIL hybrid index (or compute excess only on the equity sleeve vs equity index AND scale by equity weight); aggressive-hybrid -> CRISIL Hybrid 35+65 or NIFTY 50 Hybrid; multi-asset -> a blended index. At minimum, suppress or relabel 'skilled' for non-pure-equity categories until the benchmark is sleeve-matched.

findings.title: All 228 Sectoral/Thematic funds benchmarked to NIFTY 500, not their sector index — excess is sector beta, not skill
findings.evidence: Benchmark crosstab: Sectoral / Thematic 228/228 -> NIFTY 500. A Pharma/PSU/Defence/Transportation fund vs the broad market measures sector tilt, not selection. 18 thematic funds carry a 'skilled' verdict; thematic outliers: code 43079 ICICI PSU Equity +15.95% t=2.02 skilled; 40838 ICICI Commodities +12.92% t=2.18 skilled; 44105 HDFC Defence +25.99% (t=1.62, not yet skilled but extreme).
findings.affected: 228 thematic funds (18 of them 'skilled')
findings.fix: Map each thematic fund to its sector/thematic index (NIFTY Pharma, NIFTY PSE, NIFTY India Defence, etc.) or explicitly exclude thematic funds from skill verdicts (they already carry is_thematic=True — gate the verdict on it). Never present a thematic 'skilled' as stock-selection skill.

findings.title: 8 'skilled' verdicts rest on under-3-year histories (n_months 31-35) — confidence overstated by short-sample t-stat
findings.evidence: skilled with n_months<48: code 44072 ICICI Innovation n=31 t=2.96 excess +10.1%; 43655 HSBC Multi Cap n=34 t=2.25 +10.9%; 43335 WhiteOak Large Cap n=35 t=2.53 +4.6%; 43426 Mahindra Manulife Small Cap n=35 t=2.06 +8.3%; plus 43249/43253 (n=37), 43079 (n=38), 42443 (n=47). A monthly t-stat off ~2.6 years of holdings is fragile and easily flips with a few months.
findings.affected: 8 skilled verdicts (n_months 31-47); the engine's own years_needed field already flags inadequacy elsewhere
findings.fix: Raise the minimum-history bar for a 'skilled' label (e.g. n_months>=60 / 5y), OR down-weight the verdict to 'promising, short history' when n_months < years_needed. The engine already computes years_needed — enforce it as a gate on the skilled label.

findings.title: 2 split-scheme boundary re-codes confirmed as the ONLY duplicates; both create phantom universe rows
findings.evidence: Normalized-name dedup finds EXACTLY the 2 known pairs and no others: Kotak Large Cap (1223 n=147 skilled  + 262 n=3 insufficient) and Canara Robeco ELSS (10291 n=148 skilled + 456 n=3 insufficient). The new codes (262,456) safely degrade to 'insufficient history' so they raise no false skill claim, but they inflate the attribution universe to 768 with 2 phantom funds and the long-history twins (1223,10291) are computed on a history that ENDS 2025-07, missing the recent 3 months.
findings.affected: 2 scheme pairs (4 codes); universe count off by 2
findings.fix: Merge each pair under one stable code before attribution (stitch 1223+262 and 10291+456 into a single continuous 196-month series) so the recent 3 months feed the skill estimate and the phantom 3-month rows are dropped. This is already tracked as task #43.

findings.title: 24 gappy histories flag annualization risk; a few carry skilled verdicts
findings.evidence: gappy=True for 24 schemes (mostly the Union family with staggered launches + Quant Multi Asset 708 + Kotak Large Cap 1223 + Canara 10291 + PGIM Equity Savings 1728). Of these, 1223/10291/1728/708 plus JM Large Cap 211 are 'skilled'. n_months is materially below calendar span*12 for several, so CAGR-based excess annualization rests on a discontinuous return stream.
findings.affected: 24 schemes (5 of them skilled)
findings.fix: For gappy=True schemes, surface a UI caveat and prefer the bootstrap CI (boot_meanA_lo/hi already computed) over the point t-stat for the verdict; do not annualize across internal gaps without flagging. Cross-check the gappy series against tr_returns_monthly coverage before labeling skilled.

findings.title: Arbitrage funds (32) all benched to NIFTY 500 but safely degrade — confirm the degradation is intended, not accidental data loss
findings.evidence: All 32 Arbitrage Fund schemes resolve to verdict='insufficient history' with excess_cagr=NaN/t_stat=NaN, because the directional equity sleeve is near-zero so the attribution has no usable signal. This avoids a false 'lags by ~15%/yr' verdict, which is the correct outcome — but it means arbitrage funds carry NO skill signal at all.
findings.affected: 32 arbitrage funds (all 'insufficient history')
findings.fix: No fix needed for sanity (correct degradation), but document that arbitrage/market-neutral funds are intentionally out-of-scope for equity-selection attribution rather than 'missing data', so a future reader doesn't treat the blanks as a pipeline failure.

finding.title: 35 of 133 'skilled' verdicts are hybrid/multi-asset/balanced funds benchmarked to pure-equity NIFTY 500 — equity-sleeve excess structurally inflated
finding.evidence: Attribution measures the domestic-equity sleeve only (per the 'basis' field) but compares it to the FULL NIFTY 500. Two non-credible extremes both flagged 'skilled': code 42063 Parag Parikh Conservative Hybrid excess_cagr=+0.2351 (23.5%/yr) t=2.39; code 708 Quant Multi Asset Allocation excess_cagr=+0.2040 (20.4%/yr) t=2.70. 35 skilled funds sit in Aggressive Hybrid / Conservative Hybrid / Equity Savings / Multi Asset / Balanced Advantage categories, ALL benched to NIFTY 500. Crosstab confirms every one of these categories maps 100% to NIFTY 500.
finding.affected: 35 of 133 skilled (26%); broader 163 hybrid/arb/balanced/savings schemes all on NIFTY 500
finding.fix: Assign sleeve-appropriate benchmarks by SEBI category: arbitrage/equity-savings/conservative-hybrid -> a debt/T-bill or CRISIL hybrid index (or compute excess only on the equity sleeve vs equity index AND scale by equity weight); aggressive-hybrid -> CRISIL Hybrid 35+65 or NIFTY 50 Hybrid; multi-asset -> a blended index. At minimum, suppress or relabel 'skilled' for non-pure-equity categories until the benchmark is sleeve-matched.

verdict.reason: Independently reproduced every load-bearing number on the local files (data/funds_attribution/*.json, 768 schemes). TOTAL skilled = 133 (match). Skilled funds in non-pure-equity categories = exactly 35 under BOTH a fuzzy keyword filter and a strict exact-SEBI-category set {Aggressive Hybrid(14), Conservative Hybrid(9), Multi Asset(5), Dynamic AA/Balanced Advantage(5), Equity Savings(2)} — match. Crosstab confirmed: every hybrid/arb/balanced/savings category maps 100% to a single benchmark = NIFTY 500 (it is the ONLY benchmark any of the 176 hybrid-ish schemes receives; the only 4 non-NIFTY-500 benchmarks in the whole dataset go to pure-equity cap segments). The two named extremes reproduce exactly and are literally the #1 and #2 highest-excess skilled funds: code 42063 Parag Parikh Conservative Hybrid excess_cagr=0.2351 t=2.39 verdict=skilled; code 708 Quant Multi Asset Allocation excess_cagr=0.2040 t=2.70 verdict=skilled. The inflation MECHANISM is confirmed concrete, not just a labeling worry: the basis field on all 768 schemes explicitly states 'domestic equity sleeve only' yet the comparator is the full NIFTY 500. From the portfolio.asset_alloc block, 42063 holds only 13.0% equity (sleeve CAGR 38.6% vs NIFTY 500 15.1% = +23.5%) and 708 holds 37.7% equity (sleeve 36.8% vs 16.4% = +20.4%) — i.e. the engine credits a sleeve that is a small fraction of the fund as if it were the whole fund, against the full equity index, ignoring the 60-87% debt/cash that drags the real NAV. This cannot be a return the fund actually delivers, so the 'skilled' verdict is structurally unearned for these categories. The finding holds up under independent recomputation.
verdict.corrected: Finding is correct and reproduced; one trivial counting nit only. The finding's broader 'affected' count of 163 hybrid/arb/balanced/savings schemes is slightly understated — I count 176 such schemes (36 Dynamic AA/BAF, 32 Arbitrage, 32 Multi Asset, 30 Aggressive Hybrid, 25 Equity Savings, 19 Conservative Hybrid, 2 Balanced Hybrid), or 175 if the 2 Balanced Hybrid are excluded. This scope difference does not weaken the finding. Worth flagging as a related (non-contradicting) detail: all 32 Arbitrage funds carry verdict='insufficient history', not 'skilled' — so arbitrage does not currently feed the 35 false-skilled count, but the same NIFTY-500 mis-benchmark is latent and will mislabel arbitrage funds (which hold near-zero net equity) as soon as they accrue enough history. The fix as written (sleeve-appropriate benchmarks by SEBI category, or scale equity-sleeve excess by equity weight, or suppress/relabel 'skilled' for non-pure-equity categories) is the right remedy.

finding.title: All 228 Sectoral/Thematic funds benchmarked to NIFTY 500, not their sector index — excess is sector beta, not skill
finding.evidence: Benchmark crosstab: Sectoral / Thematic 228/228 -> NIFTY 500. A Pharma/PSU/Defence/Transportation fund vs the broad market measures sector tilt, not selection. 18 thematic funds carry a 'skilled' verdict; thematic outliers: code 43079 ICICI PSU Equity +15.95% t=2.02 skilled; 40838 ICICI Commodities +12.92% t=2.18 skilled; 44105 HDFC Defence +25.99% (t=1.62, not yet skilled but extreme).
finding.fix: Map each thematic fund to its sector/thematic index (NIFTY Pharma, NIFTY PSE, NIFTY India Defence, etc.) or explicitly exclude thematic funds from skill verdicts (they already carry is_thematic=True — gate the verdict on it). Never present a thematic 'skilled' as stock-selection skill.

verdict.reason: Reproduced every empirical claim to the decimal on the 768 attribution JSONs. (1) Sectoral/Thematic count = exactly 228; benchmark crosstab = 228/228 -> NIFTY 500. Mapping IS category-aware for size buckets (Large->NIFTY 100 x34, Mid->NIFTY MIDCAP 100 x31, Small->NIFTY SMALLCAP 250 x31, Large&Mid->NIFTY LARGEMIDCAP 250 x33), so the all-NIFTY-500 result is specific to thematics, not a global default. Across all 768 funds only 5 benchmark strings exist; NO sector index (Pharma/PSE/India Defence) appears anywhere, so a thematic fund's excess is measured vs the broad market = sector tilt, not selection. (2) 18 thematic funds carry verdict==skilled (dist: 18 skilled / 97 ahead-not-sig / 23 lagging / 89 insufficient / 1 good-selector-weak-sizer). (3) The three cited outliers match exactly: 43079 ICICI PSU excess=+0.1595 t=2.02 skilled; 40838 ICICI Commodities +0.1292 t=2.18 skilled; 44105 HDFC Defence +0.2599 t=1.62 'ahead but not yet significant' (not yet skilled). (4) excess_cagr == cagr_paper - cagr_bench exactly, and cagr_bench is the NIFTY 500 leg, so excess is genuinely vs the broad market. (5) is_thematic=True on 227/228 (lone miss 5926 ABSL International Equity is 'insufficient history', never skilled), so the proposed verdict gate on is_thematic is implementable. Finding holds in full.
verdict.corrected: Finding stands as written. One refining nuance that does NOT refute it: the engine already appends an inline caveat to all 18 skilled thematic funds' verdict_why field ('...but vs the broad market, so largely a sector bet, not pure selection'). However, the structured verdict field is still literally 'skilled', which is what a scorecard/sort/ranking surfaces, so the substantive risk (sector beta ranked/labelled as stock-selection skill) is real. Fix as proposed: map each thematic to its true sector index, or gate the 'skilled' verdict on is_thematic (covers 227/228; the 1 gap, code 5926, is harmless as insufficient-history).

audited.summary: I independently re-derived the latest-month (2025-10) portfolio viz from holdings_history.parquet + stock_industry.json using the exact shipped convention in vistas/funds_portfolio_viz.py (asset_class from investment_type; equity = nse_symbol mapped through the industry map, unmapped equity -> 'Unclassified'; sector_now = 100*sector_eq_pct/equity_pct_fund sorted desc; top holdings = equity sorted by pct-of-fund; equity_pct_fund = sum of Equity pct). For all THREE funds the shipped funds_attribution/<code>.json 'portfolio' block reproduces my numbers EXACTLY to the decimal -- zero mismatches > 0.5pp on every sector%, every top-5 holding%, equity_pct_fund, n_equity, asset_alloc and sector_cover. The viz math itself is correct and faithful. BUT the sanity layer exposes a serious upstream DATA defect that the viz surfaces misleadingly: Parag Parikh Flexi's latest snapshot discloses only 66.0% of the fund -- ALL of it tagged Equity, with NO foreign equity, NO ADR/GDR, NO cash/arbitrage rows -- even though PPFAS historically has 602 Foreign-Equity + 160 ADR rows in this same parquet. The recent feed is truncated to domestic equity only. So equity_pct_fund=66.0 and asset_alloc={'Equity':66.0} FALSELY imply ~34% of the fund is non-equity/cash, when the real fund is ~100% invested (~65% domestic + ~15% foreign equity + arbitrage/cash) that this snapshot simply omits. This truncation hits 63 pure-equity schemes (Franklin Asian Equity 34.5%, ICICI Conglomerate 46.7%, Invesco Consumption 50.6%, Samco L&M 52.1%, DSP Value 66.3%, Edelweiss Technology, the whole Quant stable 75-77%, Franklin Technology/Dividend Yield, SBI Contra) -- disproportionately foreign-heavy and Quant funds. Nippon Small Cap (95.9% disclosed) and SBI Technology (85.4%) are NOT truncated and read correctly. Style sanity: all three sector mixes match the fund's known style (PPFAS 42% Financials/HDFCBANK-led; SBI Tech 49% IT + Bharti telecom bet; Nippon broad small-cap). The 'Unclassified' band IS the genuine off-Nifty500 tail -- verified Nippon's 16.7% is 64 real small/mid-caps (Elantas Beck, AstraZeneca Pharma India, Sundram Fasteners, MAS Financial, ICRA, Fine Organic) each <0.91%, NOT a mapping failure on large names. SBI Tech 'Telecommunication'/'Services' bands are correct NSE-macro-industry classifications of real active bets (Bharti Airtel, Firstsource, Delhivery), not errors. Verdict: viz arithmetic = clean; the equity%/asset_alloc display is a 'no score for error' liability for ~63 truncated-disclosure funds.

findings.title: Latest-month disclosure truncated to domestic equity -> equity_pct_fund/asset_alloc falsely imply large cash for 63 equity funds (incl. Parag Parikh Flexi)

findings.affected: 63 pure-equity schemes (incl. 1 of the 3 audited: Parag Parikh Flexi); ~264/777 schemes disclose <90% overall
findings.fix: Do not present equity_pct_fund / asset_alloc as the fund's true asset split when total disclosed << 100. Add a 'disclosure_coverage' = sum(pct) field; if <90% (and category is pure-equity), gate or footnote the asset_alloc donut as 'partial disclosure -- foreign/cash legs not reported this month' and label sector_now explicitly as '% of DISCLOSED domestic equity'. Better: backfill the missing foreign/cash legs from the prior complete snapshot, or pull the missing rows in the loader so the feed isn't silently truncated.

findings.title: sector_now/top_holdings labelled '% of equity' / '% of fund' but are really % of the (possibly partial) DISCLOSED book
findings.evidence: sector_now normalises by eq_pct_fund (the disclosed equity sum), so for PPFAS the donut is really '% of domestic equity' not '% of equity' -- foreign equity (Alphabet/Meta/Microsoft, normally ~15% of PPFAS) is entirely absent from the sector mix, materially understating the IT/foreign tilt. top_holdings pct (% of fund) are correct as raw numbers but their relative ranking omits the undisclosed foreign top names.

Restricting to pure-equity categories (excluding Aggressive/Conservative/Balanced Hybrid, Arbitrage, Equity Savings, Multi Asset, Dynamic Asset Allocation, Childrens, Retirement) gives 557 pure-equity schemes, of which EXACTLY 63 disclose <90% -- matching the finding. audited.summary: I bridged the 767 portfolio/attribution schemes to the 566 NAV schemes the planned return-gap feature would need (return-gap = fund's actual investor return from NAV minus the return its disclosed holdings would have earned). The two sides share NO common key: the attribution _manifest carries only {name, amc} (no ISIN, no navindia_code), while scheme_master carries {name, fund_house, isin}. So the bridge is forced onto normalized name+house — ISIN, the clean join, is unavailable on the portfolio side. After normalization (lowercase, strip plan/option noise 'Direct/Regular/Growth/(G)/IDCW', expand house abbreviations SL->Sun Life, Pru->Prudential, BNP->BNP Paribas, multicap->multi cap, strip '(Formerly Known As...)' parentheticals, drop house words from the fund name, compare token-SETS within the same house): PASS A exact token-set match = 512 (66.8%); PASS B fuzzy Jaccard>=0.6 + full-containment tiers add 4; COMBINED = 516/767 = 67.3%, hitting 512 of 566 distinct NAV schemes. The 251 unmatched are NOT mostly fixable: 215 (85.7% of the residual) are STRUCTURALLY uncoverable — scheme_master is pure equity (only 'Equity Scheme - *' categories; zero Hybrid/Arbitrage/Solution-Oriented), so the unmatched Aggressive/Conservative Hybrid (49), Arbitrage (32), Multi-Asset (32), Balanced Advantage (36), Equity Savings (25), Retirement (27), Children's (12) funds have NO equity-NAV counterpart that could ever exist in this master. Only 36 unmatched are equity-category, and of those ~28 are themselves genuine absences (Dividend Yield: 0 of 10 exist in master; closed-end ELSS 'Series I-VI'/'Long Term Advantage'/'Master Equity Plan': not in the open-ended master). REALISTIC return-gap coverage ceiling on current data ~= 67-68% (516 schemes), and that is the equity sleeve essentially in full. To go higher you must change the data, not the matcher: (1) expand the NAV master to hybrid/arbitrage/solution-oriented categories (recovers ~200), and (2) stamp an ISIN or amfi_code onto the attribution manifest so the bridge becomes a deterministic key join instead of fuzzy name matching. NOTE the known split-code duplicates ride along harmlessly: Kotak Large Cap (codes 1223+262) and Canara Robeco ELSS (10291+456) each appear twice in the 767 manifest and both name-match to a single NAV scheme, so they don't hurt match rate but would double-count return-gap if not deduped to one NAV per fund identity first.
audited.metrics: portfolio_schemes=767; nav_schemes=566 (all 'Equity Scheme-*', 0 hybrid/arb/solution); shared_key=NONE (manifest has name+amc only, no ISIN/code; master has isin); exact_match(normalized name+house token-set)=512 (66.8%); fuzzy(Jaccard>=0.6)+containment add=4; COMBINED=516/767=67.3%; distinct_NAV_hit=512/566; unmatched=251 = 215 structural(non-equity, no possible counterpart) + 36 equity-category; equity-residual breakdown: ELSS=18(closed-end Series/Long-Term-Advantage, absent), Dividend-Yield=10(0 exist in master), Sectoral/Thematic=8(~half fixable formerly-known-as suffixes); house vocab gaps found: 'Helios Mutual fund' vs 'Helios Mutual Fund'(case), 'Jio Blackrock' vs 'Jio BlackRock', 'Quant' vs 'quant'(case); master-only houses: Abakkus(2); split-code dupes in 767: Kotak Large Cap 1223+262, Canara Robeco ELSS 10291+456.

verdict.reason:
I independently reproduced every load-bearing pillar of NAV-1 on the raw local files and could not refute it; the small numeric deltas are favorable to the finding, not against it.

VERIFIED FACTS:
1. scheme_master.json (the NAV master) = 566 schemes, and EVERY single one is an "Equity Scheme - *" category with ZERO Hybrid/Arbitrage/Multi-Asset/Solution-Oriented. The category counts match the finding to the unit: 255 Sectoral/Thematic, 45 Flexi, 41 ELSS, 36 Large, 36 Small, 34 Large&Mid, 33 Mid, 33 Multi, 28 Focused, 22 Value, 3 Contra. This confirms the master is equity-only and is the binding ceiling.
2. Attribution = 768 files, exactly 1 lacks a 'portfolio' block -> 767 portfolio schemes (finding's denominator, confirmed). Attribution carries NO ISIN, so name+house is genuinely the only bridge key (confirms the finding's method choice). There is no existing return-gap/nav_bridge code in the repo, so this is a proposed feature and the audit's own match is the reference computation.
3. Matcher-INDEPENDENT structural backbone: 225/767 (29.3%) portfolio schemes fall in 10 categories that the master CANNOT represent at all (Balanced Advantage 36, Arbitrage 32, Multi-Asset 32, Aggressive Hybrid 30, Retirement 27, Equity Savings 25, Conservative Hybrid 19, Children's 12, Dividend Yield 10, Balanced Hybrid 2). The finding's unmatched-by-category list (BAF 36, Multi-Asset 32, Arbitrage 32, Aggr-Hybrid 30, Retirement 27, Equity Savings 25, Conservative 19, Children's 12) matches mine on EVERY listed bucket exactly. The master-representable equity sleeve = 542/767 = 70.7%, which is the true coverage ceiling; the finding's claimed 516/767 = 67.3% sits just under it (the ~26 gap = equity funds even a good matcher misses on rebrandings, e.g. Groww/Indiabulls, UTI Master Equity).
4. Fix claim reproduced: expanding the master to hybrid/arbitrage/multi-asset/solution-oriented recovers 215 schemes (exactly the finding's "~215 structural / ~200 recoverable"), +10 dividend-yield = 225.

CAVEAT (does not change the verdict): my deliberately cruder normalizer matched only ~495 (vs the finding's 516) and left 54 'equity' residuals -- but inspection showed those are my tokenizer's failures (e.g. master 'Kotak Flexicap' vs attribution 'Kotak Flexi Cap'; 'SBI Flexicap'; 'ICICI Pru Focused' all DO exist in the master), i.e. they are recoverable by the better matcher that produced 516. This makes the structural diagnosis STRONGER, not weaker: the genuine, irreducible misses are the non-equity categories absent from the master, exactly as the finding states. The ~67% scope expectation and the "equity-only master, not match quality" root cause both hold.

verdict.reason:
I reproduced the structural facts but NOT the finding's specific evidence, and one core claim is inaccurate.

TRUE parts I independently confirmed: (1) Each _manifest.json VALUE exposes exactly 8 fields {name, amc, category, verdict, excess_cagr, t_stat, ic_t, n_months} — no isin/amfi_code field inside the value. (2) scheme_master.json carries isin for all 566 entries. (3) 767 manifest entries. (4) navindia codes and amfi codes are disjoint code spaces (0 of 767 manifest keys appear as scheme_master keys), so a direct same-key join is genuinely impossible from the current files. (5) The known duplicate-coded schemes (1223+262, 10291+456) are all present.

WHY IT DOES NOT HOLD UP: (a) FALSE premise in the title/evidence — 'attribution manifest lacks ... navindia_code'. The manifest IS a dict KEYED by navindia_code (keys '1','10070',...), and every individual attribution JSON carries an explicit navindia_code field. So the attribution side is NOT keyless; it carries a stable scheme key. The real gap is the absence of a navindia_code<->amfi_code/ISIN crosswalk file, NOT a missing key on the attribution record. (b) UNREPRODUCIBLE evidence — the concrete claims ('4 schemes only matched fuzzily', the 'ICICI Pru Value Fund vs ICICI Prudential Value Fund (erstwhile Value Discovery...)' formerly-known-as strip, 'all 767 every match is name-based') describe a NAV bridge that DOES NOT EXIST in the committed codebase. I searched every .py: the ONLY fuzzy/difflib/SequenceMatcher/token-set logic is in vistas/idmap.py, and it is STOCK-level (ISIN->NSE symbol), unrelated to scheme NAV. funds_attribution.py never references scheme_master, amfi, name-normalization, or fuzzy matching. The reported_isin/final_isin_his/vst_id in holdings_history are per-STOCK-holding identifiers, not the scheme's own ISIN, so the fix's premise that holdings 'already carries' the scheme key is also wrong. Tasks #40 (peer-NAV mapping->return-gap) and #48 (navindia->AMFI+ISIN spine) are both still PENDING — the bridge is prospective, not implemented. So the 'affected: all 767, every match name-based' and the 4-fuzzy/erstwhile specifics cannot be reproduced; they are not derivable from the data or code as it stands. Per protocol I default to refuted when the specific evidence cannot be reproduced.
verdict.corrected: The defensible, reproducible core: there is no committed deterministic key linking the attribution store to the NAV store. Concretely — _manifest.json is keyed by navindia_code (and each JSON carries navindia_code), while scheme_master.json is keyed by amfi_code and carries scheme ISIN; the two code spaces are disjoint (0/767 overlap) and NO navindia_code->amfi_code/ISIN crosswalk file exists in data/. Therefore a future NAV/peer-return bridge (task #40) has no key path and would be forced onto name+house matching unless a spine is built. The correct framing of the gap is 'missing navindia_code<->amfi_code/ISIN crosswalk' (exactly task #48, still pending), NOT 'attribution record lacks any key' (it has navindia_code). The fix is right in spirit but its mechanics are wrong: holdings_history's reported_isin/final_isin_his/vst_id are STOCK-level, not the scheme ISIN, so they cannot stamp a scheme key. The real one-time fix is to resolve navindia_code -> amfi_code (and scheme ISIN) once — via the funds_nav/AMFI NAV layer that already maps schemes — and stamp amfi_code/ISIN onto each attribution record at build time; then any NAV bridge is a 1-line key join. Severity should be downgraded to medium/low and reclassified as a forward-looking design gap (no bridge exists yet to be 'fragile'), not a high-severity defect in a live name-based bridge. The finding's per-scheme fuzzy-match evidence (4 cases, ICICI erstwhile strip) is unsubstantiated and should be dropped.
